UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, CROSS DEFENDANT HEDDI CUNDLE'S MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CAUSES OF ACTION 4-6 AS PLEAD ARISE PRIMARILY OUT OF PROTECTED ACTIVITY. LANDLORD ATTEMPTED ACCESS TO PROPERTY PURSUANT TO NOTICE UNDER CCC 1954, A PROTECTED ACTIVITY. RESPONSE TO SUCH NOTICE IS ALSO PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND/OR STATEMENTS IN ANTICIPATION OF LITIGATION. MARQUEZ DECLARATION DOES NOT PROVIDE COMPETENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF CAN PREVAIL ON THE MERITS AS TO ALLEGED STATEMENTS. THERE ARE NO DECLARATIONS FROM THIRD PARTIES TO WHOM STATEMENTS WERE MADE. SIGNED ORDER IS SERVED/MAILED TO BOTH SIDES. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, REPORTER SHELLEY MILLER, 	194 (D501)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: HEDDI CUNDLE'S Motion For Fees And Costs is continued to August 29, 2013 at 9:30am in Department 501 to provide moving party with an opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Off Calendar May-30-2013 Set For Trial On Feb-03-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jason Wolford, Esq. (415-265-2897) for Heddi Cundle, plaintiff and cross defendant / Franchesca Herrera, Esq. for Matteo Garbelotto, defendant and cross-complainant

ARGUED - CROSS DEFENDANT HEDDI CUNDLE's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is taken under submission. Plaintiff's counsel is instructed to submit proposed order conforming to the tentative ruling. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, (D501)

Court rules now adopting the tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANT HEDDI CUNDLE's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Causes of action 4-6 as plead arise primarily out of protected activity. Landlord attempted access to property pursuant to notice under CCC 1954, a protected activity. Response to such notice is also protected activity and/or statements in anticipation of litigation. Marquez declaration does not provide competent evidence to show that Plaintiff can prevail on the merits as to alleged statements. There are no declarations from third parties to whom statements were made. Signed Order is served/mailed to both sides. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Court Reporter Shelley Miller, 194 (209-551-8311), (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franchesca Herrera (415-848-8971) for Victor Marquez, defendant and moving party / Jason Wolford (415-265-2897 for Heddi Cundle, plaintiff and opposing party

ARGUED - After reviewing the pleadings and hearing the oral arguments, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT VICTOR MARQUEZ'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled - Complaint sufficiently alleges agency management ownership by moving defendant; Issue of fact improperly raised; Allegations of fraud not unintelligible. Moving party is instructed to comply with SF Local Rule 2.6(c) in the future. Order is signed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay , Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
